item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a dude that turned himself into a dog, it's a dog that temporarily turned himself into a human. Click the link in reclassification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like you missed the linked document at the end of the article.

http://www.scpwiki.com/form-5118-e-proposal-for-reclassification

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think "novice reality-bender accidentally turns itself into non-anomalous non-sapient thing" has been done, although I'd have to go back and check. Regardless, this SCP would be a lot more fun as an actual dog who kinda sorta figured out how to reality-bend, tried to become a human, and pretty much succeeded, but all it really wanted to do as a human is eat various human foods that it was normally not allowed by its owners. Imagine if it just didn't know how to properly open food containers for the foods it manifested. Maybe it eludes containment at first, but luring it with treats works and it turns out to be happy to be contained so long as it gets something resembling table scraps as part of its daily food, like it had always wanted while a pet.

item.title: +40 upvotes
item.content:encoded: Nice. Well deserved.

item.title: Re: Idea
item.content:encoded: I think you are missing the point…

item.title: Idea
item.content:encoded: I have an Idea….. Maybe include reality anchors in the containment cell so that it stays a dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked much of this but I am sorry I had to leave a downvote. The end of the containment log was enough and there wasn't much joke or added story beyond that point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Comment withdrawn, makes no sense considering the new picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a lot better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of SCP-019-JP and IMO, I think that SCP did the concept of "reality bender becomes harmless animal" a lot better. I'll have to novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you everyone! I've toned down the shock collar bit so it's more of a test and less of an act of torture. Regards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it enough to upvote, but the shock expirement is needlessly cruel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is built up far too much for what the punchline is, and all the buildup comes in entirely the wrong places. The testing table is entirely redundant, since you already give away the whole "it's just a normal dog" gambit in the containment log, and the containment log is really nothing more than a series of cartoonish sight gags. I appreciate the attempt to set up his ineptness early on, but you play that card again and again without any real differences — the orange, the bottle, the wall, the tree, the wind… it almost instantly loses any subtlety it had, and then keeps on losing it.

With a little restructuring I think this could've been a neat little joke; as it is, I get the feeling you didn't really know when to stop writing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1
As mildly amusing as the idea of a low-class reality bender turning themself into a regular dog in a panic is, it doesn't really feel like the right thing for an article. This idea feels like something you'd see in a tale, maybe as a bit of flavor detail about some researcher's dog or something, not as a classified SCP. And, beyond this, it doesn't seem to me that this article has much "meat" to it. It's sort of… empty. I didn't really feel anything reading this, just a little "huh." at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good boy :)

Good article, did not expect the ending.

Have a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think that's neccesary.

Also that would spoil the surprise that this dog is not in fact a reality bender and is in fact, just a dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it well enough, but maybe indicate the pending change in classification earlier at the top?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was absolutely brilliant and deserves my upvote, however,

I wish they didn't torture the dog at the end with the shock collar. :(
I think this skip would be better off with a less sad ending :(

What I mean is that this is a quite light hearted and fun SCP and that dark bit at the end felt quite jarring.

addressed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That image is going to give me nightmares, but whatever. Here, have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm excited to post my piece, Forever a Good Boy. I truly hope you enjoy it.

Thanks to Quadraginta and EthanHanson for helping me with their crit.

Image is from Wikimedia.
https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a2/Golden_Retriever_2.JPG/800px-Golden_Retriever_2.JPG

Please see the new tale hyperlinked in the article, or click here.
